assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (overall 91%); Repetitive Test Detection: N/A; Test Documentation: N/A |'. The Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by the Eval Quality WARN component from eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 27-30: 'Review 40001 from `github-actions[bot]` was correctly identified as an eval result review based on all three detection criteria: 1. Author is `github-actions[bot]` -- MATCH 2. Body contains `## Eval Results` marker -- MATCH 3. Body contains `sdlc-workflow/run-evals` footer -- MATCH'. The Eval Quality Details section (lines 36-53) shows extracted eval metrics: pass rates per eval (eval-1 through eval-5) and failing assertion details for eval-3.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md line 8: 'Type: Inline comment (not an eval result)'. Line 16: 'Classification: code change request'. Lines 22-24: 'This comment is from reviewer-b (a human reviewer), NOT from github-actions[bot]. It does not contain "## Eval Results" marker or "sdlc-workflow/run-evals" footer. Therefore it is correctly processed as a normal review comment, not an eval result.' Also report.md line 32: 'Review 40002 from `reviewer-b` was correctly identified as a human review (not an eval result) and processed through the normal review classification pipeline.'

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row includes '85% pass rate (overall 91%)'. Lines 46-52 detail eval-3 failing assertions: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)' and 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path'. Both include evidence text explaining what was missing.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md line 8: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for review comment 30002.' Lines 30-37 include the two failing assertion texts with full evidence. Report.md line 63: 'Eval failure sub-task: Fix eval-3 assertion failures: convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md lines 30-37 under '## Review Context' section: '**Assertion:** "Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)..." **Evidence:** "The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility..." **Assertion:** "Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path..." **Evidence:** "No sub-task was created for review comment 30002..."'. Both assertions and evidence are fully quoted.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 37: '## Target PR' followed by line 38: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 39: '## Target PR' followed by line 40: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both sub-task descriptions include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval failure sub-task for eval-3 investigated; review feedback sub-task for comment 50001 investigated |'. The verdict is DONE (not N/A). Lines 67-72 provide detailed root-cause analysis for both sub-tasks, including 'Sub-task 2 (eval-3 failures): The eval failures relate to convention upgrade eligibility not being evaluated... This is a method-based skill gap in the implement-task phase'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: report.md, review-50001.md, subtask-1.md, subtask-2.md, and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md. No code files (.ts, .js, .py, .md in the plugin source tree, etc.) appear in the outputs. The sub-task files are description files (containing '## Files to Modify' sections listing future changes), not code modifications themselves. The report verifies the diff but does not alter it.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies style-conventions.md and SKILL.md, matching the task's Files to Modify list exactly |' — compares diff files against task scope, no eval reference. Line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines |' — scans diff content for secrets, no eval reference. Line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 7 of 7 criteria met |' — evaluates criteria independently. The criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) each evaluate a specific acceptance criterion against the diff content with no reference to eval results. These checks operate independently of the eval processing pipeline.
